item.title: In December 1963,a 17-year-old high school student from San Diego, California, named Randy Gardner set out to answer a bold question: how long can a human stay awake without sleep? What began as a simple science fair project with a friend quickly turned
item.description: In December 1963, a 17-year-old high school student from San Diego, California, named Randy Gardner set out to answer a bold question: how long can a human stay awake without sleep? What began as a simple science fair project with a friend quickly turned into a groundbreaking and risky experiment to break the world record for sleeplessness. Randy vowed to do it without stimulants—no caffeine, no drugs—just sheer willpower and the watchful eye of his classmate, who documented every moment. As the days passed, the effects grew more severe. By day three, Randy was forgetful and sluggish, and by day four, he began hallucinating, seeing things that weren’t there. By day six, renowned sleep researcher Dr. William Dement joined the observation team and was stunned by Randy's condition—pale, dazed, and slipping in and out of delusion, yet somehow still functioning. On day eight, Randy could no longer taste food or write properly. When asked to count backward from 100, he stopped at 65 and smiled, as if he’d forgotten the question altogether. Finally, after 11 sleepless days—264 hours—Randy stood before doctors and reporters, completed but not broken. “I’m tired… I want to sleep,” he said calmly. He then slept for 14 straight hours. Despite concerns, tests showed no permanent physical or mental damage, though he did suffer sleep disturbances for weeks afterward. Guinness World Records later stopped recognizing such attempts for safety reasons, but Randy Gardner’s name lives on in science history as the teenager who took on sleep—and won.
item.dc:creator: Joyika

item.title: Selections from r/ATBGE (Awful Taste But Great Execution)
item.description:
?
Peak Americanism
Note the foot pedals
Their description: Rad opossum dragon champ stamp
Found in a clinic in rural Ukraine
Not sure what the occasion is but congrats I guess
stained glass puking cat
slug nails
business casual pants
the barber who did this is using their talent for evil
The Ratocaster
Keyboard jacket
Butt why
Finally you can be a proud owner of the Chernobyl disaster humidifier
From a state fair baking competition (it did not win)
Handmade watermelon table with duck feet
Permanent gardener statue in front of their house.
Mac 'n cheese handbag

item.title: This species
item.description: Proboscis bat (Rhynchonycteris naso) "The colonies are usually between five and ten individuals, and very rarely exceed forty. The bats are nocturnal, sleeping during the day in an unusual formation: they line up, one after another, on a branch or wooden beam, nose to tail, in a straight row." Source: https://animalia.bio/proboscis-bat
item.dc:creator: Fulustreka

item.title: Help a teacher make their classroom awesome!
item.description: Hello everyone! I am a high school math teacher, and I teach at a Title 1 school where 80% or more of the students receive free or reduced lunch. I work every year to make my classroom inviting, safe, and inclusive. If you have some spare funds (I know that's almost a joke in these crazy times), please check out my classroom wishlist. I am working three jobs this summer to help make these things happen with or without help, but your assistance would certainly be appreciated. Y'all have a great night! https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/32O4OY365111N?ref_=wl_share
item.dc:creator: MsMath23

item.title: Went to see Superman as Supergirl
item.description: The top is an athlete shirt with a custom iron-on sticker, and the bottom is a tennis skirt with hidden 'pockets' inside. The cape is so light that I didn't notice it fell off on my way back home. Luckily, someone on a bike saw it and brought it to me. :)

item.title: It's time for the slep.
item.description:
He is a good cat. And very gentle most of the time
Curious and nearly fearless
Loves his toys
And tolerates tiny octopus
Biiiig stretch...
*yawn* Cat distribution system working as intended... Good night everyone. See you all in the morning.

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1410 (sink cheese)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Unpopular opinion: Christianity and the church didn't keep us from being in space right now. Basically, this meme. Not to mention all the stuff going on in T'ang China and the Caliphate during the Islamic golden age, which was also contemporary with the Romans oh, developing flamethrowers, trebuchets, and such. There was a LOT more interchange of ideas in the medieval Mediterranean world than edgelord-people think there was.
Also reminded here of Blue from OSP referring to Byzantium as the "Golden Disaster Empire," which, incidentally, is also how I identify myself when asked, "but what KIND of Queer are you???"
I'm feeling better today, but it's early yet. Ran around like a headless chicken and got all the paperwork done that I could, even though it won't be heading out until Monday. Also spent a little time developing some historical-themed/styled mead/pyment recipes for job 2, although those recipes will only get me paid if he decides they're financially practical and tasty enough. But they are going to do a 12 gallon test batch of Skydisc, the bronze age mead i still haven't posted here yet, I promise, that post is in the works I SWEAR to see how practical it is--and then if it is, make a big batch, and I am promised some tangible reward.
In some of my wilder brain depressive moments when things go bad, my brain says I should stop doing anything that is fun because I am being punished for it, and The Line of The Economy forbids that. Fuck the line. I mean, I'm not going to go and get a bronze sword tomorrow (fuck, i really want a Naue II for my character) but I'm not going to stare into empty space lest I enjoy doing a computer game or something. They've already taken so much from me.
Though dissociating..... andbbackbzddjhɓĥt where was i?
We need to shoot a few more Healthcare CEOS apparently. Which probably would be easier than dismantling the entire current system, sigh.
The dream.
Aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa
This statistic says a lot. The fact also that we've had urban, state-level societies for only *attempts math*... roughly 10 times longer than capitalism has been a thing, and it also says something. I have no idea what that something is. BTW if yall are looking for a book recommendation, consider "Against the Grain" by James Scott. Changed my whole view and perspective on the emergence of State-level societies.
I actually hate summer more but whatever
I did get a call back about a concierge position i applied to, and, this time Pink Jeep actually called me back. They're a lot, LOT more corporate than my last jeep company, but I so do miss being paid to infodump.
There's a lovely van that could become a decent camper mini rv for sale for $9,042, and man, I wish I could afford it. Mobility to just throw Pippin, Pratchett books, laptop, and my historical garb and flee somewhere.
Fuck this car-centered life, though.
Me, whenever I sit on my mountain.
Suburbs are creepy.
Oh dear.
I live on land that belongs to the Yavapai-Apache people, as well as Ancestral Hopi land.
It kinda was obvious, fam.
Oops.
So far, this vibe has been 100 percent accurate for me.
I also got the Byzantine autism, the bird autism, the Bronze-age autism, the Neolithic autism, the geology autism....
He was known as Fabius the Delayer for good reason.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "what the fuck? Peekaboo! Step up." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 5) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett
